legal_frameworks.act: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012

rules.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012 - input tax credit admissible only against a registered person with a valid TIN
rules.default_suggestion: Collect a valid 12-digit TIN from the counter-party before claiming any related input VAT on Mushak 9.1.

rules.legal_reference: NBR TIN registration rules - TIN must be exactly 12 numeric digits
rules.default_suggestion: Correct the TIN to a 12-digit numeric value; verify against NBR's TIN database.

rules.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012 - standard VAT rate is 15%
rules.default_suggestion: Re-issue the invoice with VAT recomputed at the standard 15% rate, update Mushak 6.6, and re-submit Mushak 9.1 for the period.

rules.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012 - declared total must reconcile with line components
rules.default_suggestion: Reconcile the invoice total so it equals value_excl_vat + VAT, then re-submit Mushak 9.1.

rules.legal_reference: VAT & SD Rules, 2016 - returns/refunds must be processed as distinct credit notes (Mushak 6.2)
rules.default_suggestion: Reverse the negative line and process the return as a Mushak 6.2 credit note reflected in Mushak 6.6 and Mushak 9.1.

rules.legal_reference: VAT & SD Rules, 2016 - VAT credit notes must follow the prescribed credit-note procedure
rules.default_suggestion: Reverse the negative VAT line and process it via a Mushak 6.2 credit note in Mushak 9.1.

rules.legal_reference: VAT & SD Rules, 2016 - sales returns require a separate credit note, not a negative invoice
rules.default_suggestion: Reverse the negative invoice total and record the sales return as a Mushak 6.2 credit note in Mushak 6.6 and Mushak 9.1.

rules.check: invoice_no appears more than once in the same register
rules.legal_reference: VAT & SD Rules, 2016 - each Mushak 9.1 line must have a unique invoice reference
rules.default_suggestion: Renumber the duplicate invoice so each line in the register is unique, then re-submit Mushak 9.1.

rules.check: invoice_no appears in both sales and purchase registers
rules.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012, section 46 - circular or recycled invoices may lead to disallowance of input tax credit
rules.default_suggestion: Investigate the invoice appearing in both registers; if it is recycled, remove it from the offending register and file the correct transaction in Mushak 9.1.

rules.check: purchase row with positive base value but empty Mushak 6.3 reference
rules.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012, section 46 - input VAT rebate is inadmissible without the corresponding Mushak 6.3 Tax Challan reference
rules.default_suggestion: Collect the Mushak 6.3 Tax Challan reference for the purchase before claiming related input VAT on Mushak 9.1.

rules.check: filing_date is later than the 15th of the month immediately following invoice_date
rules.legal_reference: VAT & SD Rules, 2016, Rule 25 - Mushak 9.1 must be filed monthly by the 15th of the following month
rules.default_suggestion: Resubmit Mushak 9.1 within the statutory window so that invoices of the relevant month are filed by the 15th of the next month, avoiding standard statutory penalties.

filing_notes.deadline: Mushak 9.1 must be filed monthly by the 15th of the following month under the VAT & SD Rules, 2016.
filing_notes.input_vat: Input VAT on Mushak 9.1 is admissible only against a valid Mushak 6.1 Tax Invoice from a registered person with a verified 12-digit TIN, and must reconcile with the supporting Mushak 6.6 Purchase & Sales Statement.
filing_notes.treasury_reconciliation: The tax payable declared in Mushak 9.1 must equal the VAT actually deposited via valid Mushak 6.3 challans.
filing_notes.audit_risk: Mismatches may trigger a desk audit under section 53 of the Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012, and disallowance of input tax credit under section 46 of the same Act.
